text: hi, I have umbilical endometriosis. im suffering with back and leg ache too. my doctor has just diagnosed me with polymyalgia rheumatica with the aches. people over 60 get that. he wants to start me on strong steroids but im in two minds to start as I think its linked to endo. can u help me x
First mistake was going to general gynaecologist 😭. Most of them are trained enough for endometriosis

I went through the exact same. I knew they were wrong. They ordered an MRI after to prove me wrong (it wasn’t even the right MRI). They discharged me with IBS. So I paid £15k for a private surgery a year later and it took 6 hours to remove most of it and ended up staying inpatient for a week with an ovarian suspension. It was so bad there is no way they couldn’t have spotted it. I’m still so angry and I’m absolutely taking this further now x
Why do surgeons operate when they know they don’t have enough training? It seems crazy this is still going on.

i have had severe pain ever since i had my first period and alot more other symptoms when i was 9 i started seeing doctors when i was 12/13 i am now 16 been seen many times by many people iv always been told i dont have endometriosis since i have never had severe bleeding i have a mri to see if i have endometriosis hopefully soon will it always show up on mri if i do?

I have ademyosis and endo - years ago I was told scarring on POD and had 3 prem babies. constant issues with bowels and just had cameras for both ends and bowel was agony and results clear - it was said by gastro it could be behind and pushing or fused and hence why one side of my stomach /right side is large and other is small - I literally feel stuck- on wait list for laproscopy and they offered me a hysterectomy at 41 ! havent been in to see anything - i have declined and asked for new coil whilst under and to remove endo where can and 360 view @docmap

I went through laparoscopic surgery for total hysterectomy and salpingectomy because abnormal perimenopausal bleeding. Went in they said they took the tubes but hysterectomy wasn’t able to be completed due to stage 4 endo with frozen pelvis and obliterated cul de sac. Was sent home with pictures and progesterone and told if not experiencing pain to wait for menopause.😫
I had varies procedures and they couldn’t see any endo, when I had my hysterectomy the endometriosis was all over my bladder bowel gall bladder pelvic wall, they kept telling me that I didn’t have it
